paragraphs.text: Then Moses numbers the generations descended from Noah: namely, fifteen generations from Japheth, counting him in; thirty generations from Ham, also counting him in, excluding the Philistines and Cappadocians, who came forth from them afterward; twenty-seven generations from Shem, counting him in. In all the generations were seventy-two. Each of them constituted its own people and language, and each dwelt in its own land.

Of Nimrod it is said, “He was a mighty hunter before the Lord God” (Gen 10:9), because by God’s will he made war upon every tribe and forced these tribes to withdraw into the lands appointed for them by God. “Wherefore it is said: as Nimrod the mighty hunter before the Lord” (Gen 10:9) — that is, from this came the custom that, if anyone expresses a good wish to a prince or ruler, he says: “Be like Nimrod, the mighty hunter, who became famous in the wars of the Lord.” Nimrod reigned in Erech, that is in Edessa; in Accad, that is in Nineveh; in Calneh, that is in Ctesiphon; in Rehoboth, that is in Adiabene; in Calah, that is in Hetra; in Resen, or Resh-Ain, which was at that time a great city.
